req.title: Standing order
req.statement: A customer shall set up a standing order with start date in the future; past start dates shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines two equivalence partitions for start_date: valid (future) and invalid (past or today). BVA identifies today as the boundary between the two partitions, making it a critical boundary case. A positive test confirms a clearly future date schedules the order (EP). A negative test confirms a clearly past date is rejected with 422 (NEG). A boundary test confirms today's date is rejected with 422 (BVA), and an additional boundary test confirms tomorrow (today+1) is accepted (BVA). STC is applied to confirm the order transitions from non-existent to SCHEDULED state after a valid request.

tests.name: test_standing_order_future_start_date_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid bearer token
Customer has an active account eligible for standing orders
actions: Construct POST /standing-orders payload with start_date 30 days in the future
Send the request with valid Authorization header
expected: HTTP 201 Created is returned
Response body contains a standing_order_id
Response body status field equals SCHEDULED
Response body start_date matches the submitted future date
trace: R-FIN-020: start date in the future -> order scheduled
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_standing_order_future_start_date_accepted(auth_client, valid_order_payload):
    future_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()
    payload = valid_order_payload(start_date=future_date)

    resp = auth_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "standing_order_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "SCHEDULED"
    assert body["start_date"] == future_date

tests.name: test_standing_order_past_start_date_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid bearer token
Customer has an active account eligible for standing orders
actions: Construct POST /standing-orders payload with start_date 30 days in the past
Send the request with valid Authorization header
expected: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned
Response body contains an error referencing start_date
No standing order record is created
trace: R-FIN-020: past start dates shall be rejected with 422
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_standing_order_past_start_date_rejected(auth_client, valid_order_payload):
    past_date = (date.today() - timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()
    payload = valid_order_payload(start_date=past_date)

    resp = auth_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "start_date" in str(body).lower()

tests.name: test_standing_order_today_start_date_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid bearer token
Customer has an active account eligible for standing orders
actions: Construct POST /standing-orders payload with start_date equal to today's date
Send the request with valid Authorization header
expected: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned
Response body indicates start_date must be strictly in the future
No standing order record is created
trace: R-FIN-020: error pathway - 422 when start_date <= today (boundary: today == today)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date

def test_standing_order_today_start_date_rejected(auth_client, valid_order_payload):
    today = date.today().isoformat()
    payload = valid_order_payload(start_date=today)

    resp = auth_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "start_date" in str(body).lower()

tests.name: test_standing_order_tomorrow_start_date_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid bearer token
Customer has an active account eligible for standing orders
actions: Construct POST /standing-orders payload with start_date equal to today + 1 day
Send the request with valid Authorization header
expected: HTTP 201 Created is returned
Response body contains a standing_order_id
Response body status equals SCHEDULED
Response body start_date matches tomorrow's date
trace: R-FIN-020: boundary upper edge of invalid partition - today+1 must be accepted
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_standing_order_tomorrow_start_date_accepted(auth_client, valid_order_payload):
    tomorrow = (date.today() + timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = valid_order_payload(start_date=tomorrow)

    resp = auth_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "standing_order_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "SCHEDULED"
    assert body["start_date"] == tomorrow

tests.name: test_standing_order_transitions_to_scheduled_state
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid bearer token
No prior standing order exists for the given account and reference
actions: POST /standing-orders with a future start_date to create the order
GET /standing-orders/{standing_order_id} to retrieve the created order
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 with a standing_order_id
GET returns HTTP 200
Retrieved order status is SCHEDULED
Retrieved order start_date matches the submitted value
trace: R-FIN-020: order scheduled - state transition from non-existent to SCHEDULED
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_standing_order_transitions_to_scheduled_state(auth_client, valid_order_payload):
    future_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=10)).isoformat()
    payload = valid_order_payload(start_date=future_date)

    create_resp = auth_client.post("/standing-orders", json=payload)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    order_id = create_resp.json()["standing_order_id"]

    get_resp = auth_client.get(f"/standing-orders/{order_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "SCHEDULED"
    assert body["start_date"] == future_date

tests.name: test_standing_order_decision_table_all_invalid_date_forms
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid bearer token
actions: Iterate over a decision table of invalid start_date values: past date, today, empty string, null, malformed string
expected: Every request returns a 4xx HTTP status code
Past date and today return exactly 422
Empty, null, and malformed values return 400 or 422
trace: R-FIN-020: 422 when start_date <= today; invalid formats also rejected
